title: aspidium chattagrammicum
intro: Aspidium chattagrammicum, also known as the Indian Brake fern, is a species of fern native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Aspidium chattagrammicum include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and processing the fronds and roots of the fern.
